events.title: Guterres warns of ‘wider war’ as Middle East conflict enters second month
events.description: The Middle East crisis has lurched into its second month, prompting UN Secretary-General António Guterres to issue a stark warning on Thursday morning that the world is “on the edge of a wider war” with catastrophic global implications.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Guterres warns of ‘wider war’ as Middle East conflict enters second month

events.title: UN nuclear agency chief ‘deeply concerned’ by reports of latest attack on Iran power plant
events.description: Reports of yet another projectile strike near the Bushehr nuclear power plant prompted Rafael Grossi, head of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA), to register his deep concern on Saturday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UN nuclear agency chief ‘deeply concerned’ by reports of latest attack on Iran power plant

events.title: 3 gunmen open fire outside Israeli consulate in Istanbul, dubbed 'terrorists' by Turkish official
events.description: One attacker is dead and two others injured after a gunfight with police outside the Israeli consulate in Istanbul, Turkey's interior minister says.
events.ticker_label: 💥 3 gunmen open fire outside Israeli consulate in Istanbul, dubbed 'terrorists' by Turkish official

events.title: World held hostage by reliance on fossil fuels, Christiana Figueres warns – and climate health impacts are ‘mother of all injustices’
events.description: Exclusive: Former UN climate chief to co-chair Lancet Commission examining how sea-level rise is reshaping health, wellbeing and inequality What are the health impacts of sea-level rise, and who should pay? Christiana Figueres: Sea-level rise is a health crisis and we must hold polluters accountable Countries are being “held hostage” by their reliance on fossil fuels, a former UN climate chief has warned, describing the health impacts of climate change as “the mother of all injustices”. Christiana Figueres, an international climate negotiator who helped deliver the Paris agreement signed in 2016, made the comments as she was announced on Wednesday as co-chair of a Lancet Commission examining how sea-level rise is reshaping health, wellbeing and inequality . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World held hostage by reliance on fossil fuels, Christiana Figueres warns – and climate health impacts are ‘mother of all injustices’

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 6 April: Strikes persist across region as humanitarian needs rise
events.description: Strikes and counter-strikes continue across the Middle East, with dozens of casualties reported over the weekend in Lebanon following Israeli strikes targeting the south and the capital, Beirut. Meanwhile, humanitarian needs are rising, critical infrastructure remains under strain, and the wider economic and global impacts of the crisis continue to mount. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 6 April: Strikes persist across region as humanitarian needs rise

events.title: Gulf countries report attacks despite Iran ceasefire
events.description: A number of Gulf states have reported being targeted by Iranian strikes despite the ceasefire agreed between Iran, the US and Israel. Qatar and the United Arab Emirates said missiles heading for the country were intercepted, sirens were heard sounding in Saudi Arabia and two people have been injured in Bahrain due to falling debris. France 24’s correspondent Hoda Abdel-Hamid has the latest.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gulf countries report attacks despite Iran ceasefire

events.title: World News in Brief: ‘Skyrocketing’ needs outpace Sudan funding, Ukraine strikes update, global water security
events.description: The UN is significantly scaling up its presence in the Sudanese capital, Khartoum, to expand life-saving operations as the conflict between rival militaries approaches its third year.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: ‘Skyrocketing’ needs outpace Sudan funding, Ukraine strikes update, global water security

events.title: Cuba energy crisis: Humanitarian needs remain despite fuel supplies
events.description: The UN has issued an urgent call for international support as Cuba grapples with a ‘worsening’ humanitarian crisis fuelled by a prolonged energy blockade and the lingering devastation caused by Hurricane Melissa last year.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Cuba energy crisis: Humanitarian needs remain despite fuel supplies

events.title: Thailand PM urges working from home amid fears over energy crisis
events.description: Anutin Charnvirakul encourages measures such as home working and carpooling as country is reliant on oil imports Thailand’s prime minister, Anutin Charnvirakul, has called on the public to conserve energy, urging work-from-home measures and carpooling, as he warned of the impact of the conflict in the Middle East. In a statement posted on social media, Anutin said Thailand was exposed to the crisis because of its reliance on imported oil and gas, and the country could not be complacent. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Thailand PM urges working from home amid fears over energy crisis

events.title: Both sides claim victory after US, Iran agree to a two-week ceasefire
events.description: The United States and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire barely an hour before US President Donald Trump's Wednesday deadline to obliterate the country, triggering global relief. Following the announcement, both Tehran and Washington claimed to have won the more than month-long conflict.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Both sides claim victory after US, Iran agree to a two-week ceasefire

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 2 April: Civilian impact deepens as UN chief says ‘we must find a peaceful way out’ before region is engulfed by war
events.description: Deadly new strikes reported across the Middle East overnight and Thursday rattled energy markets and pushed crude oil prices up to $107 in early trading. Hopes dwindled of a quick end to the conflict as President Trump spoke of another "two to three weeks" of attacks, alongside "ongoing" discussions with Tehran. Civilians across the region continue to suffer misery and displacement. The UN chief told reporters at UN Headquarters the conflict ‘is already being felt everywhere,” and “the spiral of destruction must stop”. Follow live coverage from the Security Council here. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 2 April: Civilian impact deepens as UN chief says ‘we must find a peaceful way out’ before region is engulfed by war

events.title: Stork warning: woman gives birth midair on Jamaica-to-New York flight
events.description: Baby was delivered during Caribbean Airlines flight from Kingston to the US; nationality of child to be determined Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox A routine passenger flight from Jamaica landed at New York ’s John F Kennedy international airport with one more person than it took off with after a woman gave birth in midair, potentially setting up a tricky situation over the newborn’s citizenship. The “medical event” occurred on a Caribbean Airlines flight from Kingston on Saturday, according to a news release from the carrier. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Stork warning: woman gives birth midair on Jamaica-to-New York flight

events.title: World News in Brief: Lebanon peacekeeper deaths, Haiti mission support, drought and conflict in Somalia
events.description: Although the situation on the ground is challenging, investigations are underway into the killing of three UN peacekeepers over two consecutive days with an update expected “as soon as possible,” UN Spokesperson Stéphane Dujarric said on Wednesday.

events.title: Hungary's Orban plays on energy price fears in re-election bid
events.description: Much of Hungary's electoral campaign has centred around energy policy, at least in incumbent Viktor Orban's camp, as he seeks re-election amid the world's worst energy crisis in decades. Orban promises to keep prices low with energy subsidies and warns of unaffordable power bills should he lose. But is it really that simple? He's continued to commit to buying Russian gas, one of only two European nations still exempted from EU policy to wean the bloc off such imports, putting himself at odds with Brussels, and seeking allies elsewhere - namely in the United States.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hungary's Orban plays on energy price fears in re-election bid

events.title: Gunman killed in shootout near Israel's Istanbul consulate
events.description: Israel has said it will "not be intimidated" after an apparent failed attack on its consulate in Istanbul. One man was killed who the Turkish government says had links to a terror group.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Gunman killed in shootout near Israel's Istanbul consulate

events.title: Pope says Trump's threat to destroy Iranian civilization is 'unacceptable'
events.description: Pope Leo XIV says that U.S. President Donald Trump’s threat to destroy Iranian civilization is “truly unacceptable” and that any attacks on civilian infrastructure violate international law

events.title: WHO suspends Gaza medical evacuations after contractor killed by Israeli troops
events.description: Israel's military says troops fired at a vehicle driven by the Palestinian because they believed it posed "an immediate threat".

events.title: Oil prices fall after ceasefire deal as questions over Hormuz Strait linger
events.description: A two-week ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran on Wednesday led to a steep drop in oil prices and a rebound in Asian and European equities. In this edition, we see what the various stakeholders are saying the deal means for the future of the Hormuz Strait through which a fifth of the world's supply of oil and natural gas transits. And we see how Pakistan's adoption of solar power has partially protected it from the energy shock left in the wake of the war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Oil prices fall after ceasefire deal as questions over Hormuz Strait linger

events.title: World News in Brief: Death on the Mediterranean, displacement in Afghanistan, ‘One Health’ summit
events.description: Over 180 people are feared dead or missing in the latest shipwrecks on the Mediterranean, according to the UN migration agency, IOM.

events.title: World News in Brief: South Sudan rights, opioid guidelines update, DR Congo crisis continues
events.description: South Sudan is evolving into a catastrophic human rights and humanitarian crisis, UN Human Rights Council-appointed independent experts warned on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: South Sudan rights, opioid guidelines update, DR Congo crisis continues

events.title: Middle East crisis exposes global energy fault line as UN urges shift to renewables
events.description: The ongoing crisis in the Middle East is exposing a central vulnerability in the global economy: the dependence on fossil fuels flowing through regions affected by conflict, a situation which is strengthening the UN’s case for a faster transition to cheaper, more resilient renewable power.

events.title: Terror suspects indicted after allegedly throwing bombs at NYC protest outside mayor's mansion
events.description: The pair of terror suspects accused of trying to bomb a protest outside New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's residence in an ISIS-inspired attack have been indicted.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Terror suspects indicted after allegedly throwing bombs at NYC protest outside mayor's mansion

events.title: US embassies in Bahrain, Egypt issue warnings as Iran threatens universities across Middle East
events.description: The U.S. Embassy in Bahrain orders Americans to shelter in place, warning that Iran may target American universities in the country and across the Middle East.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US embassies in Bahrain, Egypt issue warnings as Iran threatens universities across Middle East

events.title: Iran Guards recruiting children as young as 12, putting them on front lines of war
events.description: Human Rights Watch and Amnesty International report Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps is recruiting children as young as 12 into military-linked combat and patrol roles.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran Guards recruiting children as young as 12, putting them on front lines of war

events.title: One gunman killed and two injured in shooting at Israeli consulate in Istanbul
events.description: No Israeli diplomats are currently in Turkey and the Istanbul consulate has been empty for the past two-and-a-half years.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 One gunman killed and two injured in shooting at Israeli consulate in Istanbul

events.title: As World Cup kick-off nears, a reminder of the power of sport to build bridges and break barriers
events.description: The 2026 World Cup final will take place at the MetLife Stadium in New Jersey, a few miles away from UN Headquarters where, on Wednesday, elite athletes and sports administrators spoke about the power of football and other international sports to change the world for the better.

events.title: Iran portraying ceasefire as 'victory against the United States and Israel'
events.description: Reporting from Tehran FRANCE 24’s Reza Sayah says Iranian state media is portraying the ceasefire agreement as a “victory against the United States and Israel”. Despite the heavy losses inflicted by massive strikes, the assassination of top leaders, civilian deaths, destruction of infrastructure, “the Islamic Republic is still standing,” says Sayah. “For Iran, survival was always a victory,” he notes.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran portraying ceasefire as 'victory against the United States and Israel'

events.title: As Trump threatens Iran's infrastructure, a Tehran couple wonders how to prepare
events.description: A couple living in Iran's capital have grown used to the sound of daily airstrikes five weeks into the war
events.ticker_label: 🪖 As Trump threatens Iran's infrastructure, a Tehran couple wonders how to prepare

events.title: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
events.description: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost

events.title: How Trump's threats to Iran have affected the global economy
events.description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Tomas {TAHM-us} Philipson, a former senior economic adviser in President Trump's first term, about the impact of the president's wartime threats on the global economy.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 How Trump's threats to Iran have affected the global economy

events.title: Pope Leo XIV invokes Pope Francis' final words in Easter plea against growing 'indifference' to war
events.description: In his first Easter blessing as pontiff, Pope Leo XIV called for dialogue over domination, quoting Pope Francis' warning on global indifference.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Pope Leo XIV invokes Pope Francis' final words in Easter plea against growing 'indifference' to war

events.title: News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar
events.description: Reports, based on X post from unofficial account, follow JD Vance’s accusations and threats of finding ‘legal remedies’ Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Several news outlets have falsely reported that Somaliland’s government called for the extradition of Ilhan Omar , basing their stories on a post from an X account that does not represent the state despite its claims to the contrary. Fox News, the New York Post, Sinclair Broadcast Group’s the National News Desk and the Independent ran stories on the US representative. The reports centred on a post by @RepOfSomaliland in reaction to claims by JD Vance that Omar had committed immigration fraud, which echoed prior allegations against the Somali-born Minnesota Democrat that she has vehemently denied. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar

events.title: Truck drivers warn fuel crisis could see industry ‘grind to a halt’ – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Cyclone-hammered reefs can take many years to recover, study finds Storm-ravaged coral reefs might never have the years required to recover if tropical cyclones become more intense and frequent due to climate change, marine researchers say. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Truck drivers warn fuel crisis could see industry ‘grind to a halt’ – as it happened

events.title: US, Israel and Iran agree to 2-week ceasefire as Trump pulls back on threats
events.description: Iran, the United States and Israel reached a two-week ceasefire Wednesday as U.S. President Donald Trump pulled back from his threats to destroy Iranian civilisation. The agreement came just hours before Trump's deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face attacks on its bridges and powerplants.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US, Israel and Iran agree to 2-week ceasefire as Trump pulls back on threats

events.title: Train driver dead after crash with lorry carrying military equipment
events.description: A driver was killed and 16 passengers were injured when a high-speed train and a truck carrying military equipment collided in France.

events.title: French train driver killed, 16 injured in a railway crossing crash
events.description: A French high-speed train driver has died after his train collided with a truck carrying military equipment in northern France
events.ticker_label: 💥 French train driver killed, 16 injured in a railway crossing crash

events.title: Bangladesh conducts emergency measles vaccinations as outbreak kills more than 100 children
events.description: Bangladesh is conducting emergency measles-rubella vaccinations while trying to contain an ongoing outbreak that has killed more than 100 children

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 7 April: Humanitarian and economic pressures grow; Russia and China veto resolution on Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Hostilities continue across the Middle East, with ongoing strikes, rising civilian impacts and mounting pressure on critical infrastructure and humanitarian services. On the diplomatic track in New York, China and Russia vetoed a Security Council resolution put forward by Gulf States to ensure safety and security of ships in the Strait of Hormuz - find full coverage here. Meanwhile, displacement remains high, aid access is constrained. Human rights chief Volker Türk says President Trump’s threat to annihilate Iranian civilisation is “sickening”. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 7 April: Humanitarian and economic pressures grow; Russia and China veto resolution on Strait of Hormuz

events.title: UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain report attacks despite Iran-US ceasefire
events.description: Several strikes reported across the Gulf region hours after the US and Iran announce a ceasefire for two weeks.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain report attacks despite Iran-US ceasefire

events.title: New Zealand asks US to send fuel tankers to Pacific to alleviate pressure caused by Iran war
events.description: After meeting with Marco Rubio, foreign minister Winston Peters says he made sure US understands ‘significant economic impacts on New Zealand and Pacific’ Middle East crisis – live updates New Zealand has called on the US to send fuel tankers to the Pacific to help alleviate some of the significant economic and fuel pressure caused by the war in the Middle East. Winston Peters, New Zealand’s foreign minister, met the US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, in Washington on Tuesday, where they discussed bilateral relations, the war in Iran and the Pacific. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 New Zealand asks US to send fuel tankers to Pacific to alleviate pressure caused by Iran war

events.title: US, Iran ceasefire will bring 'relief' but unclear 'what the future holds'
events.description: The two-week ceasefire agreed between the US, ISrael and Iran will bring relief for the international community and the Iranian population, Myriam Benraad, professor and Middle East specialist at the University of Exeter, tells France 24. But, with talks now set to take place to negotiate a longer-term peace and given the constradictory messages coming from the White House "we don't really know what the future holds", she says.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US, Iran ceasefire will bring 'relief' but unclear 'what the future holds'

events.title: Family pay tribute to British teenager killed in motorcycle crash in Vietnam
events.description: Orla Wates, 19, who died after incident on popular Ha Giang loop, described as ‘beautiful, independent and very funny’ The family of a British teenager have paid tribute to their daughter who died after a motorcycle crash on a popular route in Vietnam. The incident occurred on the Ha Giang loop in the country’s north, and Orla Wates, 19, died at the Viet Duc university hospital in Hanoi, according to Viet Nam News. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Family pay tribute to British teenager killed in motorcycle crash in Vietnam

events.title: #MeToo movement brings wave of harassment claims across Colombia
events.description: Female journalists’ accounts of harassment trigger avalanche of allegations reaching as far as government Juanita Gómez was reporting on an international assignment for Caracol, a Colombian television channel in 2015, when an older colleague attempted to forcibly kiss her by inside a lift. She only managed to break free from him by pushing him away several times. Fearing any complaint would come down to the word of a “girl” against that of a senior presenter, she did not report the incident. Continue reading...

events.title: India's digital census prompts fear of hidden agendas
events.description: The world's most populous country has launched a fully digital census which involves millions of enumerators and also allows the people to report their own data — but many fear it will be used for political manipulation.

events.title: 60 years after the Indonesian mass killings: Is the Cold War back?
events.description: Six decades after helping the Indonesian army crush one of the biggest communist parties in the world, the US is once again meddling in countries it considers a thorn in its flesh. Is history repeating itself?
events.ticker_label: 🪖 60 years after the Indonesian mass killings: Is the Cold War back?

events.title: Iran war: US, Israel, Tehran agree two-week ceasefire
events.description: Donald Trump has pulled back from threats to destroy Iran and agreed to a two-week ceasefire after receiving a 10-point proposal from Tehran. Iran's foreign minister has said the Strait of Hormuz would reopen. Follow DW.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: US, Israel, Tehran agree two-week ceasefire

events.title: Ukraine: Several killed as Russia strikes bus, other targets
events.description: Meanwhile, Ukrainian drone strikes on Russia and Russian-occupied regions killed at least five, including a 12-year-old boy. Ukraine has been pushing for a ceasefire during the Orthodox Easter, which is later this week.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine: Several killed as Russia strikes bus, other targets

events.title: What we know about Iran’s 10-point plan for ending war with US, Israel
events.description: Iran proposed a 10-point plan for ending the war with the US and Israel early Wednesday after the establishment of a two-week ceasefire just before a US-imposed deadline and threats to destroy Iranian "civilisation". US President Donald Trump said the plan was "workable", despite several points which Washington had previously said were unfeasible.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 What we know about Iran’s 10-point plan for ending war with US, Israel

events.title: US-Iran ceasefire ‘a major problem domestically’ for Netanyahu
events.description: Reporting from Jerusalem, FRANCE 24’s Noga Tarnopolsky says the US-Iran ceasefire is likely to be “a major problem domestically” for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu. The right-wing Israeli prime minister’s base “was expecting regime change, was expecting something very dramatic” with the Iran war. The two-week ceasefire agreement falls short of their expectations, Tarnopolsky notes.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US-Iran ceasefire ‘a major problem domestically’ for Netanyahu

events.title: Bahrain sounds its missile alert alarm hours after U.S. and Iran said they agreed on a 2-week ceasefire in the war
events.description: Bahrain sounds its missile alert alarm hours after U.S. and Iran said they agreed on a 2-week ceasefire in the war
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Bahrain sounds its missile alert alarm hours after U.S. and Iran said they agreed on a 2-week ceasefire in the war

events.title: Security Council: Russia and China veto resolution on Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The UN Security Council has failed to adopt a resolution aiming to boost security in the Strait of Hormuz as the critical shipping corridor remains largely closed to global trade and the transport of humanitarian aid, with war continuing to rage across the region.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council: Russia and China veto resolution on Strait of Hormuz

events.title: Women leaders unite to advance gender equality, defend multilateralism amid growing global pushback
events.description: The Secretary-General’s commitment towards women leadership in the United Nations was recognized at a pivotal moment marked by global uncertainty, economic volatility and increasing pressure on hard-won rights.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Women leaders unite to advance gender equality, defend multilateralism amid growing global pushback

events.title: Breaking the Gaza aid bottleneck: 106-tonne delivery arrives via new sea route
events.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has facilitated the delivery of some 106 metric tonnes of lifesaving nutrition supplies to the Gaza Strip – the first shipment via a mechanism to deliver aid by sea, in line with a UN Security Council resolution and amid the ongoing war in the Middle East.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Breaking the Gaza aid bottleneck: 106-tonne delivery arrives via new sea route

events.title: US-Israeli war on Iran: What is happening on day 40?
events.description: The US and Iran agree to a two-week truce, just an hour before Trump’s deadline to escalate the conflict expired.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US-Israeli war on Iran: What is happening on day 40?

events.title: U.S. and Iran agree to 2-week ceasefire
events.description: As part of the agreement, set to take effect immediately, Trump said the U.S. and Israel would suspend bombing Iran for two weeks, subject to Iran following through on its commitment to reopen the Strait of Hormuz for safe passage during the ceasefire period.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 U.S. and Iran agree to 2-week ceasefire

events.title: Trump has backed down from his threat to wipe out Iran's civilization
events.description: President Trump has backed down from his threat to wipe out Iran's civilization and bomb its power plants by Tuesday night. Online, he said he agreed to suspend the bombing of Iran for two weeks.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Trump has backed down from his threat to wipe out Iran's civilization

events.title: How Pakistan brokered a two-week ceasefire deal between Iran and the US
events.description: Pakistan cast itself as an unlikely diplomatic broker on Wednesday, saying it had helped secure a two-week ceasefire between Iran and the United States. Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif also said he would host talks in Islamabad aimed at ending the Middle East war beginning on April 10.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 How Pakistan brokered a two-week ceasefire deal between Iran and the US

events.title: Former Australian soldier charged with committing 5 war crime murders in Afghanistan
events.description: A former Australian soldier has been charged with war crimes on allegations that he killed five unarmed Afghans while serving in Afghanistan from 2009 and 2012
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Former Australian soldier charged with committing 5 war crime murders in Afghanistan

events.title: Gunmen attack building housing Israeli Consulate in Istanbul
events.description: Turkish officials say that gunmen attacked a building housing the Israeli Consulate in Istanbul, sparking a gunfight that left one assailant dead and two police officers wounded

events.title: Daylight hammer attack suspect is illegal alien released under Biden policies: DHS
events.description: Haitian illegal immigrant Rolbert Joachim allegedly bludgeoned a mother to death with a hammer outside a Florida gas station. DHS says he was released under the Biden administration.

events.title: Violent mob of juveniles swarms streets, attacks officers in wild ‘teen takeover’ caught on video
events.description: Washington, D.C., authorities took juveniles into custody after teen takeovers allegedly led to street brawls and assaults on police officers Saturday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Violent mob of juveniles swarms streets, attacks officers in wild ‘teen takeover’ caught on video

events.title: Mauro compares Iran rescue of missing colonel to Maduro capture, credits intelligence preparation
events.description: Paul Mauro says U.S. intelligence agencies had already laid the groundwork needed to locate and rescue a missing colonel from enemy territory in Iran.

events.title: Iran executes people, including teens, by hanging
events.description: The nation of Iran has been carrying out executions, including of teenagers, as the U.S. and Israel wage war against the country, reports indicate.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran executes people, including teens, by hanging

events.title: Iran, proxy militias threaten US universities in Lebanon as Americans urged to flee now
events.description: Iran and its proxy militias have threatened universities in Lebanon, U.S. officials say, and the State Department warned Americans to depart immediately.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran, proxy militias threaten US universities in Lebanon as Americans urged to flee now

events.title: GCC, other Middle East nations react to Iran-US ceasefire announcement
events.description: Countries in the region welcome the temporary truce and urge negotiations for the war's permanent end.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 GCC, other Middle East nations react to Iran-US ceasefire announcement

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Tropical Cyclone Vaianu may bring life-threatening winds to New Zealand, forecasters warn
events.description: Category 3 cyclone is moving south of Fiji towards New Zealand, with winds at centre in excess of 150km/h Tropical Cyclone Vaianu forming in the Pacific could bring life-threatening winds and heavy rain to New Zealand later this week, forecasters have said, with strong wind watches issued for the entire North Island. The category 3 cyclone is moving south of Fiji towards New Zealand, with winds around the centre in excess of 150km/h, MetService said on Wednesday. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Tropical Cyclone Vaianu may bring life-threatening winds to New Zealand, forecasters warn

events.title: Daughter of missing American woman in Bahamas says there were 'prior issues,' calls for full investigation
events.description: The daughter of a missing American in the Bahamas calls for intensive investigation, saying prior issues have been brought to her attention about the case.

events.title: World welcomes US-Iran ceasefire, urges lasting peace in the Middle East
events.description: Countries urge full commitment to two-week ceasefire, praise Pakistan for role in facilitating truce.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World welcomes US-Iran ceasefire, urges lasting peace in the Middle East

events.title: US seeks to deport Kilmar Ábrego García to Liberia despite new Costa Rica deal
events.description: Man born in El Salvador has been fighting removal to series of ‘third’ countries after mistaken deportation last year US government attorneys on Tuesday told a federal judge the Department of Homeland Security still intends to deport Kilmar Ábrego García to Liberia, despite a new agreement with Costa Rica to accept deportees who cannot legally be returned to their home countries. The Salvadorian national’s case has become a focal point in the immigration debate after he was mistakenly deported to El Salvador last year. Since his return, he has been fighting a second deportation to a series of African countries proposed by homeland security officials. Continue reading...

events.title: Uganda receives first US deportation flight under third-country agreement
events.description: Dozen people arrive under new deal but legal challenges expected with scheme criticised as ‘dehumanising process’ A flight carrying people being deported from the US has landed in Uganda, as Donald Trump’s administration pushes on with its strategy of expelling migrants to countries they have no ties to. The deported people would stay in the east African country as “a transition phase for potential onward transmission to other countries”, an unnamed senior Ugandan government official told Reuters. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Uganda receives first US deportation flight under third-country agreement

events.title: Commonwealth leaders vow to keep seeking reparations after Reform UK plan to halt visas
events.description: Politicians warn party’s pledge to ‘punish’ countries seeking justice for slavery will harm and isolate Britain Commonwealth politicians say they will not back down from seeking reparations as UK public figures, including a former Reform insider, warn the rightwing party’s pledge to “punish” countries seeking justice for slavery would harm and isolate Britain. This week, Reform UK said they would halt visas for nationals of countries formally demanding reparations from Britain if they took power. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Commonwealth leaders vow to keep seeking reparations after Reform UK plan to halt visas

events.title: ICE involved in shooting after agency says illegal immigrant gang member tried to ram officer
events.description: Federal agents in California shot a suspected gang member wanted in an El Salvador murder case after he allegedly tried to run over an ICE officer.
events.ticker_label: 💥 ICE involved in shooting after agency says illegal immigrant gang member tried to ram officer

events.title: What we know about the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran
events.description: The provisional truce comes more than a month after the US and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 What we know about the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran

events.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon after US-Iran ceasefire
events.description: Air strikes hit the Tyre and Nabatieh areas hours after the US-Iran ceasefire was announced.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel strikes southern Lebanon after US-Iran ceasefire

events.title: Oil prices plunge and shares jump on US-Iran ceasefire plan
events.description: Crude prices tumbled by as much as 15% on the conditional pause but remain far higher than before the war.

events.title: US-Iran ceasefire deal: What are the terms, and what’s next?

Traoré took power in a coup in September 2022 , toppling another junta that had taken power just nine months earlier. He has since stifled opposition and in January banned political parties outright. Continue reading...

events.title: First Nation asks court to block Alberta referendum on seceding from Canada
events.description: Sturgeon Lake Cree Nation has asked a court to halt the separatist push, arguing it would violate their treaty rights A First Nation in Alberta has said that a separatist push for the province to secede from Canada is “consummately irresponsible and dishonourable” and should be shut down, arguing in court that a proposed referendum would violate their treaty rights. A minority of residents of the oil-rich province have long argued that the province’s woes are due to the structure of payments to the federal government and a perceived inability to get their vast fossil fuel reserves to market. Continue reading...

events.title: Ben Roberts-Smith to remain in jail after bail hearing over war crimes charges

events.title: Row over ‘virtual gated community’ AI surveillance plan in Toronto neighbourhood
events.description: Rosedale residents considering car licence plate-scanning Flock system in bid to tackle property crime A row has broken out in one of Canada’s wealthiest neighbourhoods over plans to use an AI-powered surveillance system to create the country’s first “virtual gated community” to combat surging property crime. Crime rates in Toronto as a whole are dropping but residents of Rosedale have been left on edge by a sustained rise in home invasions, with robbers targeting the tree-lined neighbourhood at a rate more than double the city average. Break-ins and thefts remain the third highest per capita in Toronto. Continue reading...

events.title: ‘We still deserve due process,’ says Cambodian man deported by US to Eswatini
events.description: Pheap Rom was one of 15 people sent to prison in African kingdom last year despite completing US sentences A Cambodian man deported by the US said he would have accepted being sent to Cambodia, but instead ended up imprisoned in Eswatini, a country he knew so little about that when he first read the name he thought it was another immigration detention centre in Louisiana. Pheap Rom, who had been convicted of attempted murder, was one of 10 deportees sent to Eswatini by the US in October 2025. They joined a group of five men, from Cambodia, Cuba, Jamaica, Vietnam and Yemen, who were deported to the small southern African country in July. All were sent to a maximum-security prison. Rom was deported from Eswatini to Cambodia in March. Continue reading...

events.title: Rapper Offset in hospital after being shot in Florida
events.description: The rapper is in a "stable" condition, with two people detained in connection with the incident.

events.title: China probes ‘malicious’ cyberbullying of teen diving champion Quan
events.description: Three-time Olympic gold medallist has faced toxic online abuse and considered quitting over comments on her appearance.

events.title: Meloni’s Trump trouble: Why Italian PM is distancing herself from US leader
events.description: The Italian leader's cosy alliance with Trump may have become more of a liability than an asset as key elections near.

events.title: Romanian football great Mircea Lucescu dies aged 80
events.description: Lucescu, one of the most successful Romanian football coaches and players, won several European club titles.

events.title: ‘Our geography is our oil’: Why Djibouti hosts many foreign military bases
events.description: Just miles from one another, along Djibouti's coast, sit military bases of rival powers including the US and China.

events.title: On rare China visit, Taiwan’s opposition leader calls for reconciliation
events.description: KMT chief Cheng Li-wun, the party's first leader to visit China in a decade, hopes to meet Chinese President Xi Jinping.

events.title: ‘Fearless’ teen sensation Sooryavanshi aces Bumrah test in IPL blitz
events.description: The 15-year-old opener hit 39 off 14 balls as Rajasthan Royals put on 150-3 in 11 overs against Mumbai Indians.

events.title: In Strait of Hormuz, Iran and China take aim at US dollar hegemony
events.description: Tehran and Beijing both stand to gain from boosting status of the Chinese yuan.

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
events.description: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

events.title: Congressional Democrats raise alarm over Trump's comments on Iran
events.description: Dozens of congressional Democrats raised alarm Tuesday over President Trump's rhetoric about Iran. Most Republican lawmakers have been silent.

events.title: Taiwan's opposition leader arrives in China for a 'Journey of Peace'
events.description: The visit takes place ahead of President Trump's own summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping next month, where Taiwan is expected to be a top agenda item.

events.title: Taiwan opposition leader makes rare visit to China
events.description: During a visit to a memorial for Taiwan's founding father in Nanjing, KMT leader Cheng Li-wun called for "reconciliation and unity" with Beijing. Taiwan's ruling democrats say China needs to stop its aggression.

events.title: Should Western travel influencers be promoting Afghanistan?
events.description: In the race for clicks, likes and followers, some travel influencers are being criticized for promoting Afghanistan while ignoring the harsh reality of Taliban rule.

events.title: Turkey: DW correspondent Alican Uludag remains in custody
events.description: DW correspondent Alican Uludag has been in pre-trial detention in Istanbul for six weeks, charged with 'insulting the president.' His lawyers say this is completely disproportionate.

events.title: Afghanistan, Pakistan held 'useful' peace talks, Kabul says
events.description: Clashes between the Afghan Taliban and Pakistan reignited in February. Pakistan accuses Afghanistan of harboring hostile militants, which Kabul denies. China has offered to mediate in easing tensions.

events.title: Virtual Romanian singer goes viral but sparks criticism
events.description: In Romania, an AI-generated singer inspired by poems about the Roma community has become an overnight success. But critics say her creator is monetizing the woes of a marginalized group without involving its members.

events.title: Artemis II: Crew snaps 'Earthset' in throwback to Apollo 8
events.description: The Artemis II astronauts photographed the moment when the Earth dips below the moon's horizon, as they prepare to return to our planet's gravitational pull.

events.title: UK denies Kanye West entry, music festival canceled
events.description: US rapper Ye, formerly called Kanye West, has been denied entry into the UK over his numerous antisemitic outbursts. He had been scheduled to headline a music festival that has now been canceled.
events.ticker_label: 💥 UK denies Kanye West entry, music festival canceled

events.title: Champions League: Bayern win in Madrid and eye semifinal spot
events.description: Bayern Munich won 2-1 away at Real Madrid in the first leg of the Champions League quarterfinals. The second leg will take place in Munich on April 15.

events.title: Trump's surprise claim in his call with Sky News
events.description: 👉 Follow Trump100 on your podcast app 👈

events.title: Greece to ban under-15s from social media
events.description: Greece will ban under-15s from social media from next year.

events.title: Scottish 'crime boss' extradited after Bali arrest
events.description: An alleged Scottish crime boss wanted by Spain has been extradited from Bali, Indonesian authorities have confirmed.

events.title: Afghanistan says peace talks held in China to end fighting with Pakistan have been constructive
events.description: Afghanistan’s Foreign Ministry says peace talks held in China between Afghanistan and Pakistan to halt cross-border fighting between the two neighbors have been constructive

events.title: Sarkozy says he owes France 'the truth' as he challenges conviction
events.description: Former French President Nicolas Sarkozy has maintained his innocence on conspiracy charges in an appeal hearing in Paris

events.title: Taiwan opposition leader heads to China in what she calls a ‘journey for peace’
events.description: Taiwan opposition leader Cheng Li-wun has arrived in China at the invitation of President Xi Jinping, in what she's calling a “journey for peace” as Beijing pushes for the self-governed island to come under its control

events.title: Texas man tackled by church security after bringing loaded gun, ammo to Houston service
events.description: Police say a Texas man armed with a gun and more than 100 rounds of ammunition was tackled by a security guard inside a Houston church before shots.

events.title: First-ever photo of Earth from moon’s far side unveiled as Artemis II begins journey home, Trump weighs in
events.description: NASA unveils historic 'Earthset' photo taken by Artemis II astronauts showing Earth setting behind the lunar surface from the moon's far side.

events.title: ICE nabs 5 illegal immigrants wanted for murder abroad in New England crackdown
events.description: ICE says it arrested five illegal immigrants in New England who are wanted for murder and violent crimes in their home countries, including in Brazil.

events.title: Iryna Zarutska mural moved to new Providence location after pressure campaign from mayor, activists
events.description: A Providence restaurant owned by immigrants is now displaying a mural honoring slain Ukrainian refugee Iryna Zarutska after left-wing backlash.

events.title: Iran's UN ambassador takes swipe at Trump in final hours before Strait of Hormuz deadline
events.description: Iran's UN ambassador is calling one of President Donald Trump's posts on Truth Social on Tuesday “deeply irresponsible" and “profoundly alarming."

events.title: Inside Iran’s ruling ideology: How a ‘holy mission’ and messianic doctrine fuel regime extremism
events.description: Iranian journalist says Iran's regime uses messianic Mahdi doctrine to claim divine authority and make political compromise nearly impossible.
